markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: The latest delay pushing Grand Theft Auto VI to November 19, 2026 has widened the window for traders to assess which major developments will precede the Rockstar title. Market-implied odds reflect strong consensus around a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire and Drake’s Iceman album dropping first, driven by ongoing peace talks and the artist’s confirmed project timeline. Other outcomes such as GPT-6’s release sit at lower implied probabilities, tracking AI industry roadmaps and OpenAI announcements. With the market closing July 31, 2026, upcoming catalysts include any fresh Rockstar updates on polish or marketing, alongside chart performance and geopolitical headlines that could shift momentum in this high-volume entertainment prediction market.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent developments in Harvey Weinstein’s legal cases have driven trader consensus toward limited additional incarceration. The May 2026 mistrial on the remaining New York rape charge—following a 2025 mixed verdict and the 2024 overturn of his original 23-year sentence—highlighted ongoing prosecutorial hurdles and jury skepticism over witness credibility. Weinstein, 74, continues serving time on his separate Los Angeles conviction, which his team is actively appealing on similar fairness grounds. These repeated courtroom setbacks, combined with his age and documented health concerns, have elevated market-implied odds for no further prison time while compressing probabilities for longer sentences. Upcoming appellate rulings and any potential retrial scheduling remain key catalysts that could shift sentiment in this closely watched entertainment-industry accountability case.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Multiple NHL clubs head into the 2025-26 season with balanced rosters and realistic championship paths, keeping implied probabilities tightly grouped among the top contenders. The Colorado Avalanche draw support from a proven core and regular-season consistency, while the Carolina Hurricanes emphasize defensive structure and special-teams execution. Vegas Golden Knights rely on goaltending depth and prior title experience, and the Montreal Canadiens benefit from emerging talent and improved standings. Traders weigh schedule difficulty, injury resilience, and historical playoff patterns across these teams, resulting in a competitive field where no single franchise holds a decisive edge.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Vegas Golden Knights win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Multiple NHL clubs head into the 2025-26 season with balanced rosters and realistic championship paths, keeping implied probabilities tightly grouped among the top contenders. The Colorado Avalanche draw support from a proven core and regular-season consistency, while the Carolina Hurricanes emphasize defensive structure and special-teams execution. Vegas Golden Knights rely on goaltending depth and prior title experience, and the Montreal Canadiens benefit from emerging talent and improved standings. Traders weigh schedule difficulty, injury resilience, and historical playoff patterns across these teams, resulting in a competitive field where no single franchise holds a decisive edge.

markets.question: Will the Oklahoma City Thunder win the 2026 NBA Finals?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Oklahoma City Thunder win the 2026 NBA Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder's league-best 64-18 regular-season record and defending-championship pedigree anchor their 49.5% implied probability, though a hamstring issue for forward Jalen Williams adds uncertainty heading into Game 3 of the Western Conference finals against the San Antonio Spurs. The Spurs sit at 62-20 and have closed the gap to 30.6% after splitting the first two games, leveraging depth and recent momentum despite guard De'Aaron Fox's ankle concern. In the East, the New York Knicks' 53-29 mark and 2-0 series lead over the Cleveland Cavaliers support their 19.7% standing, reflecting strong home form and roster continuity. The Cavaliers trail at 2.1% amid a tougher path despite their 52-30 finish. These probabilities reflect trader consensus on current playoff positioning, injury timing, and historical conference-final patterns.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The tightly bunched probabilities among the leading contenders reflect the depth of European talent and the absence of any single dominant side entering the 2026 tournament. France and Spain sit at the front on the strength of recent Nations League results, experienced squads, and favorable qualifying paths, while England’s solid form and tactical consistency keep it within striking distance. Brazil and Argentina maintain respectable positions through historical pedigree and star quality, yet neither has pulled clear amid inconsistent recent performances. The even spread across the top tier underscores how minor shifts in fitness, manager decisions, or late qualifying outcomes could quickly reshape the field before the opening matches.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party through ongoing military leadership purges and elite reshuffles ahead of the 21st Party Congress scheduled for late 2027, with no verified challenges to his position emerging in recent months. His May 2026 summits with U.S. President Trump and Russian President Putin in Beijing further signaled uninterrupted authority, as he hosted back-to-back state visits while advancing diplomatic priorities on Taiwan and trade. Recent leadership changes have targeted subordinates without affecting the paramount leader, consistent with patterns of consolidation since term limits were removed in 2018. These developments align with trader consensus that structural and institutional barriers make an exit before 2027 highly unlikely.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination years away, traders assign Gavin Newsom the highest implied probability at 24.1 percent, reflecting his California governorship, national fundraising network, and visibility in swing-state outreach. The next tier—Kamala Harris at 9.3 percent, Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez at 8.8 percent, and Jon Ossoff at 6.2 percent—illustrates a fragmented field where no contender exceeds one-quarter support. Key differentiators include prior electoral records, legislative experience, and alignment with varying voter coalitions in battleground states. Early primaries, donor consolidation, and polling trends in key states could narrow the field by clarifying which candidates demonstrate broad appeal versus regional strength.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination years away, traders assign Gavin Newsom the highest implied probability at 24.1 percent, reflecting his California governorship, national fundraising network, and visibility in swing-state outreach. The next tier—Kamala Harris at 9.3 percent, Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez at 8.8 percent, and Jon Ossoff at 6.2 percent—illustrates a fragmented field where no contender exceeds one-quarter support. Key differentiators include prior electoral records, legislative experience, and alignment with varying voter coalitions in battleground states. Early primaries, donor consolidation, and polling trends in key states could narrow the field by clarifying which candidates demonstrate broad appeal versus regional strength.
